summary:
Great! The test is now passing. The issue was that the CsvReader was using ISO-8859-1 encoding to read the file, but the file was actually UTF-8 encoded. By changing the encoding to UTF-8, we've fixed the issue and all the non-ASCII characters are now being read and displayed correctly.

The fix was to:
1. Change the charset in CsvReader from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8
2. Import the StandardCharsets class to use the UTF_8 constant

This ensures that the international characters in names and cities are properly preserved throughout the processing pipeline.
